```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
prev <- sample(seq(0.01,0.10,0.01),1); sens <- sample(seq(0.80,0.98,0.02),1)
esp <- sample(seq(0.85,0.99,0.02),1)
ans <- sens*prev/(sens*prev+(1-esp)*(1-prev))
vals <- round(c(ans,sens,esp,prev,1-ans),4); opts <- sprintf("$%.4f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Uma condição tem prevalência `r sprintf("%.2f",prev)`. Um teste possui sensibilidade `r sprintf("%.2f",sens)` e especificidade `r sprintf("%.2f",esp)`. Qual é $P(\text{condição}\mid\text{positivo})$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
Pelo teorema de Bayes,
$$P(C\mid+)=\frac{P(+\mid C)P(C)}
{P(+\mid C)P(C)+P(+\mid C^c)P(C^c)}
=`r sprintf("%.4f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S4Q4_teorema_Bayes
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
